introduction.heading: Introduction
introduction.text: This experiment explores how light waves interfere when they pass through a double slit and compare with a diffraction grating.
list_items: Use the observed fringe spacing to connect the pattern to wavelength and slit spacing.
Record the wavelength setting used for each trial.

principle.heading: Experiment Principle
principle.text: Constructive and destructive interference produce bright and dark fringes whose spacing depends on geometry and wavelength.

procedure.heading: Experimental Procedure
procedure.text:
1. Align the laser with the slit assembly.
2. Place the screen at the marked distance.
3. Adjust the slit width and capture the fringe pattern.
list_items: Turn on the laser and verify the beam is centered on the slit.
Move the screen until a clear fringe pattern appears.
Capture one photo for each configuration.

observations.heading: Required Observations
observations.text: Observe how the fringe spacing changes when the slit width or screen distance changes.
list_items: Note whether the central maximum is sharp or broadened.
Record any misalignment or stray-light conditions.

analysis_cues.heading: Analysis Cues
analysis_cues.text: Compare the measured fringe spacing with the theoretical prediction and explain any discrepancies.
list_items: Use the table values to estimate the wavelength.
Discuss whether uncertainty comes mainly from alignment or reading the fringe spacing.

thinking_questions.heading: Thinking Questions
list_items: Why does the fringe spacing shrink when the screen is moved farther away?
What would change if the laser wavelength were different?
